preview_text:
The model favors England heavily, assigning an 87% win probability against Ghana's near-zero chance. This represents a commanding expected advantage for England; Ghana's implied path to avoid defeat rests almost entirely on a 13% draw probability according to the model.

The win-draw-loss spread reveals a contest the model views as substantially one-sided. England's 87% win share dwarfs Ghana's modeled probability, and while a draw carries meaningful 13% weight, the baseline expectation is a decisive England victory rather than a closely contested affair.

The market consensus—both sportsbooks and Polymarket—departs meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks assign Ghana a 7% win chance versus the model's 0%, and edge Ghana's draw probability to 15%. This 7-percentage-point gap on Ghana's win line represents the most material divergence. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view is correct; the model may be overconfident in ruling out Ghana, or the market may be pricing in uncertainty the model's inputs do not capture.
